commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Protected maintainer PR with current-main drift, dirty mergeability, and broad upgrade/security/automation surfaces needs human refresh and merge review rather than an automated repair lane.; Cleared: Security-sensitive areas were sampled and no concrete security or supply-chain defect was found; the DOMPurify upgrade and boundary-touching code still need maintainer intentionality before merge. commands.review_summary:
The PR refactors hot-path markdown, Codex streaming, transcript/session, media, channel/plugin, provider, workflow, and test surfaces to reduce repeated scans and redundant I/O, while also updating DOMPurify and release/maintainer automation helpers.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a maintainer performance/refactor PR rather than a single bug report. Review used PR evidence, live GitHub state, source inspection, scoped policy, and Codex contract inspection instead of a reproduction path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer PR with current-main drift, dirty mergeability, and broad upgrade/security/automation surfaces needs human refresh and merge review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: improve: reduce hot-path linear scans and redundant I/O
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Several high-frequency paths repeatedly scanned arrays, moved worklists from the front, or re-read filesystem metadata. Those patterns made common workloads quadratic or multiplied I/O as transcript, stream, media, and memory data grew.

## Why This Change Was Made

- process markdown chunk worklists as stacks instead of repeated `shift`/`unshift`
- retain tool-block indexes while streaming instead of calling `indexOf` for each fragment
- tail-scan modern parent-linked transcripts and preserve the full scan for legacy/mixed files
- reuse loaded image buffers and dimensions instead of re-reading metadata
- resolve local media roots once per attachment batch, after managed inbound exemptions
- deduplicate repeated short-term memory source checks within a promotion batch

The changes keep ordering, validation, legacy behavior, and transport semantics intact. No speculative persistence/index format was added.

## User Impact

Large markdown responses, parallel tool streams, long transcripts, image batches, and repeated memory sources do less redundant work. The expected result is lower CPU and filesystem overhead on these hot paths without changing output or security boundaries.

## Evidence

- focused Vitest run: 6 shards, 555 tests passed on the exact PR tree
- follow-up SDK surface, plugin contract, and media checks passed
- `oxfmt --check` and `git diff --check` passed
- fresh autoreview: clean, overall patch correctness `0.86`
- markdown benchmark with 50,000 chunks: approximately `263 ms` baseline versus `69 ms` after the worklist change
- transcript benchmark with 20,000 parent-linked entries: approximately `42 ms` baseline versus `22 ms` after the tail scan
- hosted CI run `27948310315` passed all 50 jobs before the latest mainline-only rebase
- hosted CI run `27948998212` has 49 passing jobs; its only failure is three untouched `src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts` tests, reproduced on exact `origin/main` at `f40071cc`
target.author: vincentkoc
